dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3276 IS: UNtaxed Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Lee introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations

form.official-title: To provide for limitations on the implementation and enforcement of a global carbon tax with respect to the United States.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the UNtaxed Act.

section.header: Limitation on United Nations taxes, tariffs, fees, or other such penalties
section.text: No tax, tariff, fee, or other such penalty may be levied upon United States citizens or United States entities by the United Nations or any organ, specialized agency, commission, or other formally affiliated body of the United Nations, unless such tax, tariff, fee, or other such penalty is imposed under a treaty with respect to which the Senate has provided its advice and consent under clause 2 of section 2 of article II of the Constitution of the United States.

section.header: Limitation on funds relating to global carbon taxes
section.text: No funds are authorized to be appropriated or otherwise made available for—

paragraph.text: assessed or voluntary contributions of the United States to the United Nations or any United Nations-affiliated body that would be used to impose a global carbon tax; or
implementation or enforcement of such a global carbon tax.
The term global carbon tax means a tax imposed under a global fuel regime that—

subparagraph.text: requires the owner or operator of a vessel to reduce the level of greenhouse gases emitted by the vessel; and
imposes such a tax at set costs with respect to such level of emission of greenhouse gases.

paragraph.text: The term United States entity means an entity organized under the laws of the United States or any jurisdiction within the United States.
